w20: Memory-optimized server featuring 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM, designed for large-scale, memory-intensive enterprise database and data processing workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server with 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM, designed for large-scale, memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server featuring 40 vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM. It provides a stable environment for large-scale, memory-intensive enterprise database applications.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring an x86_64 Intel Xeon architecture. It provides 40 vCPUs across 20 physical cores with a clock speed of 2.2 GHz, supported by 961 GB of RAM, resulting in approximately 24.02 GB of memory per core. This configuration includes no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core performance across various metrics, including PassMark CPU and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, while single-core performance and specific tasks like gzip compression rank in the bottom 10 percent. Memory bandwidth metrics, including read-only and write operations, consistently fall within the average range. The server architecture supports essential instruction sets such as AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA, alongside hypervisor-based virtualization. Given the high memory-to-core ratio and stable multi-core throughput, this server is suitable for large-scale, memory-intensive enterprise database operations and similar data-heavy workloads.
